title: Cross-Platform App Icon & Packaging Image Specifications
description: Comprehensive specification of icon/image sizes, shapes, formats, and locations for all major platforms, packaging formats, and distribution channels. Derived from Apple HIG, Android/Material Design guidelines, Microsoft docs, freedesktop.org spec, Bjango Templates, JReleaser docs, and platform developer portals.

masterSourceRecommendation.description: Start from a single 1024x1024 square SVG or PNG master image. Do NOT pre-apply rounded corners or masks — platforms apply their own masking. No transparency for App Store / Play Store icons.

apple-ios-ipados.label: Apple iOS & iPadOS
apple-ios-ipados.description: iOS and iPadOS app icons. Apple applies a superellipse (squircle) mask automatically — provide flat square images with no transparency. iOS 26+ uses Icon Composer with up to 5 layers at 1024x1024.

apple-ios-26-plus.label: Apple iOS & iPadOS 26+
apple-ios-26-plus.description: Starting with iOS 26, Apple uses Icon Composer format with up to 5 layers (tinting, dark mode, etc.). Only a single 1024x1024 source is needed per layer.

icons.notes: Multi-layer: auto-generates all sizes. Tinting and dark mode variants supported.

apple-imessage.label: Apple iMessage
apple-imessage.description: iMessage app icons are non-square with rounded corner masks applied by the system.

apple-macos.label: Apple macOS
apple-macos.description: macOS app icons use a rounded rectangle shape (not masked by the OS — you design the rounded corners). macOS 26+ uses Icon Composer with automatic masking. Pre-macOS 26: transparency is allowed, you design the full icon including rounded corners and shadow.

icnsBundle.description: A single .icns file contains all sizes above as a bundle

apple-macos-26-plus.label: Apple macOS 26+
apple-macos-26-plus.description: macOS 26+ uses Icon Composer with automatic masking. Rounded corners applied by the system. Up to 5 layers (dark, tinted variants). Transparency not allowed.

apple-watchos.label: Apple watchOS
apple-watchos.description: watchOS app icons are circular — Apple applies a circular mask automatically. Provide square images.

apple-tvos.label: Apple tvOS
apple-tvos.description: tvOS icons have subtle rounded corners applied by the system. 5-layer parallax icons with depth.

apple-visionos.label: Apple visionOS
apple-visionos.description: visionOS icons are circular, masked by the system. Uses Icon Composer with up to 5 layers.

android-legacy.label: Android Legacy Launcher Icons
android-legacy.description: Pre-Android 8.0 (API 25 and below) launcher icons. Single-layer square icons. Masking varies by launcher.

android-adaptive.label: Android Adaptive Icons (API 26+)
android-adaptive.description: Adaptive icons use two layers (foreground + background) on a 108dp canvas. The visible area is a 72dp circle centered within the 108dp canvas. OEM launchers apply different masks (circle, squircle, rounded square, teardrop). Safe zone: keep important content within 66dp center circle. Android 13+ adds an optional monochrome layer for themed icons.

safeZone.description: Content within the 66dp diameter circle centered on the 108dp canvas is guaranteed visible across all launcher masks

monochromeLayer.description: Android 13+ (API 33) themed icon layer. Single-color silhouette that the system tints.
monochromeLayer.sizes: Same as adaptive icon layers above
monochromeLayer.notes: Provide as single-color (typically black on transparent) — system applies user-chosen tint color

android-notification.label: Android Notification Icons
android-notification.description: Notification icons must be white silhouettes on transparent background. The system applies coloring based on user theme.

windows-classic.label: Microsoft Windows (Classic Win32)
windows-classic.description: Windows application icons provided as multi-resolution ICO files. The ICO format embeds multiple PNG images at different sizes. Design as square with transparent background — Windows applies its own visual treatment.

icoBundle.description: A single .ico file containing multiple embedded PNG images

icons.use: Context menu, title bar, system tray

windows-uwp-tiles.label: Microsoft Windows UWP Tiles
windows-uwp-tiles.description: UWP/WinUI tile icons for Start Menu. Multiple scale factors required. Visual Studio can auto-generate from a single 1024x1024 source.

linux-freedesktop.label: Linux (Freedesktop hicolor icon theme)
linux-freedesktop.description: Freedesktop.org Icon Theme Specification. Icons placed in hicolor theme directory structure. Desktop environments handle any masking. SVG is preferred as it renders at any size.

icons.notes: Preferred format — renders at any size

macos-dmg.label: macOS DMG (Disk Image)
macos-dmg.description: DMG disk images require a volume icon (ICNS) and optionally a background image for the Finder window when mounted.

icons.notes: Hidden file, set with SetFile -a V

windows-msi.label: Windows MSI (WiX Toolset)
windows-msi.description: MSI installers require an ICO file for Add/Remove Programs and optional BMP images for custom installer dialogs.

flatpak.label: Flatpak
flatpak.description: Flatpak follows the freedesktop hicolor icon spec. Icons placed in the export directory. Flathub prefers 256x256 PNG minimum or SVG. JReleaser requires at least one icon, 256x256 preferred.

snap.label: Snap / Snapcraft
snap.description: Snap packages need a single icon file referenced in snapcraft.yaml. The Snap Store applies a circular mask in listings. SVG or 512x512 PNG recommended.

appimage.label: AppImage
appimage.description: AppImage requires a .DirIcon PNG file at the root of the AppImage filesystem for desktop integration. Minimum 128x128, 256x256+ preferred.

jpackage-linux.label: JPackage (Linux deb/rpm)
jpackage-linux.description: JPackage for Linux packages uses a single PNG icon. jpackage embeds it into the .desktop file and hicolor theme directories automatically.

jpackage-macos.label: JPackage (macOS dmg/pkg)
jpackage-macos.description: JPackage for macOS requires an ICNS file containing all required resolutions.

jpackage-windows.label: JPackage (Windows msi/exe)
jpackage-windows.description: JPackage for Windows requires an ICO file containing all required resolutions.

chocolatey.label: Chocolatey
chocolatey.description: Chocolatey packages reference an icon via a publicly accessible HTTPS URL. No icon is embedded in the package. PNG format, typically 256x256.

jreleaserConfig.notes: Must be publicly accessible HTTPS URL

homebrew.label: Homebrew (Cask)
homebrew.description: Homebrew Cask uses the macOS app bundle's existing ICNS icon. No separate icon file is required in the formula. An optional icon URL can be provided.

icons.notes: Optional — uses app bundle icon by default

favicon.label: Web Favicon
favicon.description: Favicon images for web browsers. Modern browsers support SVG favicons. Legacy ICO format embeds multiple sizes. Apple and Microsoft have additional tile/bookmark sizes.

pwa.label: Progressive Web App (PWA)
pwa.description: PWA manifest icons. Maskable icons require safe zone padding: content must fit within the inner 80% circle. Non-maskable icons fill the full square.

safeZone.description: For maskable icons (purpose: 'maskable'), content must fit within the inner 80% circle of the icon. The outer 10% on each side may be cropped.

chrome-extension.label: Chrome / Browser Extension
chrome-extension.description: Chrome extension icons declared in manifest.json under the 'icons' key. Other Chromium-based browsers follow the same spec.

vscode-extension.label: VS Code Extension
vscode-extension.description: VS Code extension icon for the Marketplace. Declared in package.json under the 'icon' key.

jetbrains-plugin.label: JetBrains / IntelliJ Plugin
jetbrains-plugin.description: JetBrains Marketplace plugin icon. SVG is the preferred format. Declared in plugin.xml or marketplace listing.

safeZoneSummary.description: Platforms that require safe zone / padding to ensure content visibility after masking

zones.reason: OEM launchers apply different masks (circle, squircle, rounded square, teardrop) that crop outer edges
Different browsers apply different mask shapes; content outside 80% may be cropped
Apple applies a superellipse (squircle) mask — corner areas are cropped but less aggressively than Android
Snap Store applies a circular mask in listings

dedupedOutputSizes.description: The unique set of PNG sizes needed to cover ALL platforms above. Generate these from your 1024x1024 source, then assemble into ICO/ICNS bundles as needed.

scalableFormats.description: Scalable vector graphic — preferred for Linux, JetBrains, and modern web
